critic_raw:
The experiment uses Precision as the primary metric to 'minimize false positives,' but the model selection process optimizes Precision during hyperparameter tuning without pairing it with a minimum Recall constraint. A degenerate model that predicts no defects at all achieves perfect Precision — this degenerate solution satisfies the primary metric maximally while being operationally useless. The design does not specify a threshold, a minimum acceptable Recall, or a combined metric (F-beta) that would prevent this degenerate collapse during validation-guided model selection.

Stratified random splitting on images from an operational facility over two years disregards temporal drift in defect distribution. Shipping container damage patterns shift over time with fleet aging, inspection protocol changes, and seasonal loading patterns. The design itself acknowledges 'seasonal variations and potential changes in inspection protocols' as confounds, then addresses them with 'stratification checks across time periods' — but stratification checks after a random split do not constitute temporal holdout. The test set contains random samples from all time periods rather than strictly future observations.

Anomaly detection on industrial images is characterized by a severe minority class (defects are rare relative to normal containers). The design states class imbalance is a concern but does not describe the positive rate in the dataset. If the defect rate is under 1%, precision and recall become highly sensitive to small threshold changes, and aggregate metrics like F1 averaged across classes are dominated by the majority class. Reporting strategies for rare-event anomaly detection (precision-recall AUC, average precision) are not mentioned.

The traditional baseline is described as 'traditional image processing techniques' without specifying what those are. Classical methods for industrial defect detection span a wide range — simple thresholding, edge detection, template matching, or pretrained classical detectors. Without operationalizing the baseline, 'equal tuning effort' is undefined and the comparison cannot be replicated.

The operational scenario involves a single imaging setup (shipping facility), meaning the CNN likely learns facility-specific lighting signatures and camera angle artifacts rather than generalizable defect morphology. No hold-out from a different facility or camera setup is proposed to test this.
all_issues_raised: Precision-only optimization during model selection admits the degenerate solution of predicting no defects (perfect Precision, zero Recall), making the primary metric maximizable by a useless model without any stated Recall floor or F-beta constraint.

Temporal drift in defect patterns (fleet aging, protocol changes) is acknowledged as a confound but addressed only with post-hoc stratification checks on a randomly-split test set — not a temporal holdout — so the test set is not a true forward-looking evaluation.

The defect positive rate is unspecified; at typical industrial anomaly rates (under 1–2%), F1 and precision/recall values are highly threshold-sensitive and aggregate metrics become dominated by the majority non-defect class, requiring precision-recall AUC rather than point metrics.

The traditional baseline is unspecified ('traditional image processing techniques') — without defining the feature extraction and detection algorithm, 'equal tuning effort' cannot be operationalized and the comparison is not reproducible.

The model is trained and evaluated on a single facility's images; facility-specific lighting and camera artifacts may be the dominant learned signal rather than generalizable defect morphology, and no cross-facility evaluation is proposed.
all_issues_adjudicated: Precision-only optimization during model selection admits the degenerate solution of predicting no defects (perfect Precision, zero Recall), making the primary metric maximizable by a useless model without any stated Recall floor or F-beta constraint.

Temporal drift in defect patterns (fleet aging, protocol changes) is acknowledged as a confound but addressed only with post-hoc stratification checks on a randomly-split test set — not a temporal holdout — so the test set is not a true forward-looking evaluation.

The defect positive rate is unspecified; at typical industrial anomaly rates (under 1–2%), F1 and precision/recall values are highly threshold-sensitive and aggregate metrics become dominated by the majority non-defect class, requiring precision-recall AUC rather than point metrics.

The traditional baseline is unspecified ('traditional image processing techniques') — without defining the feature extraction and detection algorithm, 'equal tuning effort' cannot be operationalized and the comparison is not reproducible.

The model is trained and evaluated on a single facility's images; facility-specific lighting and camera artifacts may be the dominant learned signal rather than generalizable defect morphology, and no cross-facility evaluation is proposed.
